,18/11/2024,18/11/2024 USG ABDOMEN AND PELVIS,DR.JOSHI DEVASHREE,SURGERY

USG ABDOMEN AND PELVIS,18/11/2024,Dr. KELKAR DHANANJAY,Clinical profile : Ca anal canal.

Liver is normal in size and texture. No focal mass is seen. Hepatic veins and portal vein are normal.

Gall bladder is well distended. Its walls are thin. No intrinsic pathology is seen. CBD is normal. No intrahepatic biliary dilatation is seen.

Pancreas is normal in size and echotexture.

Both kidneys are normal in size and texture. Corticomedullary differentiation is well maintained. No evidence of hydronephrosis or calculus is seen. A 36 mm left lower pole cortical cyst is seen in left kidney.
Right kidney : 8.9 x 4 cm Left kidney : 8.7 x 4.7 cm

Spleen is normal in size and texture.
No evidence of lymphadenopathy or ascites is seen.
Visualized intra abdominal bowel loops are normal in caliber and show peristalsis. No obvious bowel wall thickening is seen.

Urinary bladder is well distended. Its walls are thin. No intrinsic pa

MRI OF PELVIS- ANORECTAL REGION
,18/11/2024,KELKAR DHANANJAY,
Pelvis was scanned with 3-4 mm thin slices in orthogonal planes. T1WI, T2W1 images were obtained with and without fat saturation. Post contrast imaging was also obtained.

Clinical profile : Ca anal canal. scopy -anal verge growth. HPE - WD adenoca. P/R -growth at 2 cm, anterior wall, 1/3 circumference.

There is heterogeneously enhancing asymmetric circumferential wall thickening noted at lower rectum extending across ano-rectal junction to involve upper anal canal.
Significant restricted diffusion seen in view of significant ADC hypointensity.
It involves the submucosa and the muscularis propria layer with significant thinning and few patchy subtle areas of breach in the muscularis.
No exophytic component is seen in mesorectal fascia.

The length of involvement is 4.1 cm and starts approximately at a distance of 2.5 cm from the anal verge with maximum thickness of approximately 0.8 cm.

Dr. PINGLEY SONALI,Ca Ano-rectum,Colonoscopy (11.11.24)--growth at anal verge
HPE--well differentiated adenocarcinoma

USG AP (18.11.24)--No significant abnormality is noted.

MRI Anorectal (18.11.24)
There is heterogeneously enhancing asymmetric circumferential wall thickening noted at lower rectum extending across ano-rectal junction to involve upper anal canal.
Significant restricted diffusion seen in view of significant ADC hypointensity.
It involves the submucosa and the muscularis propria layer with significant thinning and few patchy subtle areas of breach in the muscularis.
The length of involvement is 4.1 cm and starts approximately at a distance of 2.5 cm from the anal verge with maximum thickness of approximately 0.8 cm.
It involves lower rectum predominantly from 11 to 7 o'clock position. Involvement of internal anal sphincter and anal mucosa is seen from 8-12 o'clock position.
This lesion causes mild narrowing of the lumen. Associated mild perilesion

An ulcero-infiltrative tumor measuring 3.5 x 3 x 0.8 cm is seen, below anterior peritoneal reflection & involving the dentate line. It involves the bowel partly in anterior quadrant. The tumor invades the wall of colon rectum up to muscle layer.

Circumferential resection margin (CRM) is grossly free of tumor and is 3 cm away from the tumor.
Rest of the colon is unremarkable.

Resection margins:
Tumor is 12 cm from the proximal and 3 cm from the distal resection margin.
CRM is 3 cm away from the tumor.
